text: Hey team, as we scale our new Agentic AI platform, we need to decide on our cloud provider strategy for Q3. We are currently split between AWS and GCP. GCP has better native AI tools, but our legacy system is entirely on AWS.
Moving our legacy databases from AWS RDS to GCP Cloud SQL sounds like an absolute nightmare right now. The migration downtime alone would violate our enterprise SLAs.
But if we stay on AWS, integrating Vertex AI models will require custom middleware that we don't have the bandwidth to build. GCP's native Gemini integrations will save us 3 weeks of dev time.
What if we adopt a multi-cloud approach? Keep the data layer on AWS RDS, but host the compute and LLM orchestration on GCP via Anthos?
Multi-cloud latency between AWS east and GCP east is around 12ms. For an agentic query engine, that's acceptable. Good compromise.

Okay, decision made. We will move forward with a multi-cloud architectural pattern. AWS will retain the primary data stores (RDS/S3), while GCP will handle all AI inference and Agent orchestration. Sam, please draft the Anthos sizing docs by Friday.
